If the Imam عليه السلام knew that he would sleep in the Prophet's bed and rise safely in the morning, it would not be considered such a great honor, and what has been mentioned in the verses of the Quran and narrations regarding the importance of this sacrifice would not seem as justified.

Yes, the issue of "volitional knowledge" is an answer to all such objections.

5- There is another way of reconciliation for the various narrations on the knowledge of the Unseen (though this way applies only to a portion of these narrations), which is that the addressees in these narrations were different. To those who had the capacity and readiness to accept the issue of the knowledge of the Unseen regarding the Imams, the truth of the matter was told. But before opponents or those who were weak and of low capacity, the speech was presented according to the listener's understanding.

For example, we read in a Hadith that Abu Basir and several of the great companions of Imam al-Sadiq عليه السلام were in a gathering. The Imam entered the gathering in a state of anger. When he sat down, he said in the presence of the group:

"How strange it is that some people claim we know the Unseen! None knows the Unseen except Allah, the Mighty and Majestic. I intended to discipline my maidservant, so-and-so, but she fled from me, and I did not know in which of the rooms of the house she was!!" [1] The narrator of the Hadith says: "When the Imam عليه السلام rose from the gathering, I and some other companions of the Imam entered the inner part of the house and said: 'May we be sacrificed for you, you said such a thing about your maidservant, while we know you possess much knowledge, and we do not use the term "knowledge of the Unseen"?'"

The Imam عليه السلام then gave an explanation in this regard, the concept of which was his awareness of the secrets of the Unseen.

It is clear that in that gathering, there were individuals who did not have the necessary readiness and capacity to perceive these meanings and the knowledge of the Imam's status.

It should be noted that these five ways are not mutually exclusive, and all of them can be true (take note).

[1]: Usul al-Kafi, Vol. 1, Chapter: Rare mention of the Unseen, Hadith 3.

2- Another Way to Prove the Knowledge of the Unseen of the Leaders

There are two other ways to prove the reality that the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Infallible Imams عليهم السلام were generally aware of the secrets of the Unseen.

First: We know that the scope of their mission was not limited to a specific place or time; rather, the mission of the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imamate of the Imams عليهم السلام is universal and eternal. How is it possible for someone to have such an extensive mission while having no awareness except of his own limited time and environment? Can someone who is, for example, commissioned to govern a vast portion of a country be ignorant of that region and yet perform his mission well?!

In other words: The Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imam عليه السلام, during their lifetimes, must explain and implement the Divine laws in such a way that they answer the needs of all humans in every time and place, and this is not possible unless they know at least a portion of the secrets of the Unseen.

Second: There are three verses in the Holy Quran which, if placed side by side, clarify the issue of the knowledge of the Unseen and the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله and the Imams عليهم السلام. First, regarding the one who brought the throne of the Queen of Sheba to Solomon in the blink of an eye (i.e., Asaf bin Barkhiya), the Quran says: قالَ الَّذِي عِنْدَهُ عِلْمٌ مِنَ الْكِتابِ أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ يَرْتَدَّ إِلَيْكَ طَرْفُكَ فَلَمَّا رَآهُ مُسْتَقِرًّا عِنْدَهُ قالَ هذا مِنْ فَضْلِ رَبِّي: "One who had knowledge of the Book said: I will bring it to you before your gaze returns to you. And when (Solomon) saw it placed before him, he said: This is by the grace of my Lord" (Naml: 40).

In another verse we read: قُلْ كَفى‌ بِاللَّهِ شَهِيداً بَيْنِي وَ بَيْنَكُمْ وَ مَنْ عِنْدَهُ عِلْمُ الْكِتابِ: "Say: Allah is sufficient as a witness between me and you, and he who has knowledge of the Book" (Ra'd: 43).

On the other hand, in multiple hadiths narrated in Sunni and Shi'a books, we read that Abu Sa'id al-Khudri says: "I asked the Messenger of Allah صلى الله عليه وآله about the meaning of 'the one who had knowledge of the Book' (al-ladhi 'indahu 'ilmun min al-kitab). He said: 'He was the successor of my brother Solomon bin David.' I asked: 'Who is "the one who has knowledge of the Book" (wa man 'indahu 'ilmu al-kitab)?' He said: 'That is my brother Ali bin Abi Talib!'" [1] Considering that "knowledge from the Book" ('ilmun min al-kitab) mentioned regarding Asaf refers to "partial knowledge," and "knowledge of the Book" ('ilmu al-kitab) mentioned regarding Ali عليه السلام refers to "total knowledge," the difference between the scientific status of Asaf and Ali عليه السلام becomes clear.

Thirdly, in verse 89 of Surah Al-Nahl we read: وَ نَزَّلْنا عَلَيْكَ الْكِتابَ تِبْياناً لِكُلِّ شَيْ‌ءٍ: "And We have sent down to you the Book as an exposition of everything."

It is clear that one who is knowledgeable of the secrets of such a Book must know the secrets of the Unseen, and this is a manifest proof that it is possible for a human from the Awliya of Allah to become aware of the secrets of the Unseen by the command of Allah.

Regarding the knowledge of the Unseen, we have had other discussions under verses 50 and 59 of Surah Al-An'am (Vol. 5, pp. 245 and 268) and verse 188 of Surah Al-A'raf (Vol. 7, p. 46).

[1]: Refer to Vol. 3 of Ihqaq al-Haqq, pp. 280-281 and Nur al-Thaqalayn, Vol. 2, p. 523.

3- Research into the Creation of the "Jinn"

"Jinn," as derived from the linguistic concept of the word, refers to an invisible being for whom many characteristics are mentioned in the Quran, including:

1- It is a being created from a flame of fire, unlike man who was created from dust (وَ خَلَقَ الْجَانَّ مِنْ مارِجٍ مِنْ نارٍ - Ar-Rahman: 15). 2- It possesses knowledge, perception, the ability to distinguish truth from falsehood, and the power of logic and reasoning (various verses of Surah Al-Jinn). 3- It has religious obligations and responsibility (verses of Surah Al-Jinn and Surah Ar-Rahman). 4- A group of them are righteous believers and a group are disbelievers (وَ أَنَّا مِنَّا الصَّالِحُونَ وَ مِنَّا دُونَ ذلِكَ) (Jinn: 11). 5- They have a gathering, a resurrection, and a hereafter (وَ أَمَّا الْقاسِطُونَ فَكانُوا لِجَهَنَّمَ حَطَباً) (Jinn: 15). 6- They had the power to penetrate the heavens and gather news by eavesdropping, but were later barred (وَ أَنَّا كُنَّا نَقْعُدُ مِنْها مَقاعِدَ لِلسَّمْعِ فَمَنْ يَسْتَمِعِ الْآنَ يَجِدْ لَهُ شِهاباً رَصَداً) (Jinn: 9). 7- They would establish contact with some humans and, with the limited knowledge they had of certain hidden secrets, would engage in seducing humans (وَ أَنَّهُ كانَ رِجالٌ مِنَ الْإِنْسِ يَعُوذُونَ بِرِجالٍ مِنَ الْجِنِّ فَزادُوهُمْ رَهَقاً) (Jinn: 6). 8- Among them are individuals who possess great power, just as there are among humans (قالَ عِفْرِيتٌ مِنَ الْجِنِّ أَنَا آتِيكَ بِهِ قَبْلَ أَنْ تَقُومَ مِنْ مَقامِكَ): "A powerful one among the jinn said to Solomon: I will bring the throne of the Queen of Sheba to you before you rise from your place!" (Naml: 39). 9- They have the power to perform certain tasks needed by humans (وَ مِنَ الْجِنِّ مَنْ يَعْمَلُ بَيْنَ يَدَيْهِ بِإِذْنِ رَبِّهِ‌ ...يَعْمَلُونَ لَهُ ما يَشاءُ مِنْ مَحارِيبَ وَ تَماثِيلَ وَ جِفانٍ كَالْجَوابِ): "A group of jinn worked before Solomon by the permission of his Lord... they made for him whatever he willed of places of worship, statues, and basins like reservoirs" (Saba: 12-13). 10- Their creation on earth was before the creation of humans (وَ الْجَانَّ خَلَقْناهُ مِنْ قَبْلُ) (Hijr: 27), along with other characteristics.

Furthermore, it is clearly understood from the verses of the Quran that, contrary to what is famous among the common folk who consider them "better than us," the human being is a species superior to them. This is because all Divine Prophets were chosen from among humans, and the jinn believed in the Prophet of Islam, who was of the human species, and followed him. Fundamentally, the obligation of prostration before Adam upon Iblis—who, according to the explicit text of the Quran, was of the jinn (Kahf: 50)—is proof of the superiority of the human species over the jinn.

Up to this point, the discussion has been about matters derived from the Holy Quran regarding this invisible being, which is free from any superstition or unscientific issues. However, we know that the common and uninformed people have fabricated many superstitions about this being that do not align with reason and logic. This has given this being a superstitious and illogical image, such that when the word "jinn" is mentioned, a bundle of superstitions is brought to mind—including depicting them with strange, bizarre, and terrifying shapes, as beings with tails and hooves!, harmful and harassing, vengeful and ill-behaved, who might set houses on fire just because a container of hot water was poured in an empty spot!, and other such delusions.

However, if the subject of the existence of jinn is purged of these superstitions, the core matter is completely acceptable. For we have no reason to limit living beings to what we can see; rather, scientists and naturalists say: the beings that man can perceive with his senses are negligible compared to the beings that cannot be perceived with the senses.

Until recently, when microscopic living organisms had not been discovered, no one believed that in a drop of water or a drop of blood, there could be thousands upon thousands of living beings that man lacks the power to see.

Scientists also say: our eyes see limited colors, and our ears hear limited sound waves; the colors and sounds that cannot be perceived by our eyes and ears are far more than those that can be perceived.

When the state of the world is such, what room is there for surprise that there are types of living beings whom we cannot see?

Another point worthy of attention is that "jinn" is sometimes applied to a broader concept that includes various types of invisible beings, whether they possess intellect and perception or not. Even a group of animals that are seen with the eye but usually hidden in nests are included in this broad meaning.

The witness for this is a narration from the Prophet صلى الله عليه وآله who said:

"Allah created the jinn in five categories: a category like the wind in the air (invisible), a category of snakes, a category of scorpions, a category of insects of the earth, and a category like the children of Adam who are subject to reckoning and punishment." [1] Considering this narration and its broad concept, many of the problems mentioned in narrations and stories regarding jinn will be solved.

For example, in some narrations from the Commander of the Faithful Ali عليه السلام, we read that he said:

"Do not drink water from the broken part of a vessel nor from its handle, for the devil sits upon the handle and the broken part." [2] Considering that the "devil" (Shaytan) is from the "jinn," and considering that the broken part of a vessel, as well as its handle, is a place for the accumulation of various microbes, it does not seem unlikely that "jinn and Shaytan" in the "general sense" also include such organisms, even though it has a specific meaning referring to a being possessed of understanding, consciousness, responsibility, and obligation.

The narrations in this field are numerous. [3]

O Lord! On that day when jinn and men are present in Your court of justice and the evildoers are all regretful of their deeds, place us under the shadow of Your grace.

O Allah! The domain of Your kingdom is vast and expansive, and our knowledge and recognition are limited; protect and preserve us from slips, errors, and judging without truth.

O God! The status of Your Prophet is so high that his call was answered not only by humans but also by the jinn; place us among the believers in his call.

Amen, O Lord of the Worlds. End of Surah Al-Jinn. Friday, 21st of Muharram al-Haram, 1407 (September 26, 1986).
